examples: Contemporary theories of reinforcement learning are rooted in the dopaminergic reward system.
Continuous dopaminergic drug delivery is an unmet medical need in advanced Parkinson's disease.
Parkinson's disease is characterized by the death of dopaminergic neurons in areas of the midbrain.
In one experiment Walsh and his team injected mice with a toxin, called MPTP, that induces Parkinson's disease by killing off dopaminergic neurons.
